perturbation.original_text: 12.2 Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement if the other party ----------- materially breaches a material obligation hereunder and such breach remains uncured for thirty (30) days following the notice to the breaching party of the breach and the notifying party's intention to terminate. All undisputed payments that have accrued prior to the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason will be payable in full within thirty (30) days thereof. In addition, upon the termination of this Agreement by Client for any reason, a pro-rata amount of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be refunded to Client calculated as follows: the Monthly Payment Fee less the cost of the Impressions displayed for such month as of the effective date of the termination, calculated on an average CPM basis.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2 Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement if the other party ----------- materially breaches a material obligation hereunder and such breach remains uncured for thirty (30) days following the notice to the breaching party of the breach and the notifying party's intention to terminate. All undisputed payments that have accrued prior to the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason will be payable in full within thirty (30) days thereof. In addition, upon the termination of this Agreement by Client for any reason, no refund of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be provided under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the termination clause to state that 'no refund of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be provided under any circumstances' upon termination by the Client, it directly contradicts the original clause which specified a pro-rata refund of the Monthly Payment Fee, calculated based on Impressions displayed until termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2 Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement if the other party ----------- materially breaches a material obligation hereunder and such breach remains uncured for thirty (30) days following the notice to the breaching party of the breach and the notifying party's intention to terminate. All undisputed payments that have accrued prior to the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason will be payable in full within thirty (30) days thereof. In addition, upon the termination of this Agreement by Client for any reason, no refund of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be provided under any circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 12.2 to state that 'no refund of the Monthly Payment Fee shall be provided under any circumstances' upon termination by the Client. This directly contradicts the original language in the same section (and duplicated in the 'Contradicted Text') that specified a pro-rata refund of the Monthly Payment Fee, calculated based on Impressions displayed until termination. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the client's entitlement to a refund upon termination.

perturbation.original_text: 18.1 Assignment. Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, ---------- without the other party's written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed); provided however, that either party may assign its rights and obligations hereunder in the event of a sale of all, or substantially all of such party's assets related to this Agreement, whether by merger, reorganization, operation of law or otherwise, or (2) either party's assignment and/or delegation of its rights and responsibilities hereunder to a wholly-owned subsidiary or joint venture in which the assigning party holds an interest. Any attempt to assign this Agreement other than as permitted above will be null and void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of both parties, their successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 18.1 Assignment. Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, ---------- without the other party's written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed); provided however, that either party may assign its rights and obligations hereunder in the event of a sale of all, or substantially all of such party's assets related to this Agreement, whether by merger, reorganization, operation of law or otherwise, or (2) either party's assignment and/or delegation of its rights and responsibilities hereunder to a wholly-owned subsidiary or joint venture in which the assigning party holds an interest. Notwithstanding the first sentence of this Section 18.1, Intuit may freely assign this Agreement to any third party without the consent of Client. Any attempt to assign this Agreement other than as permitted above will be null and void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of both parties, their successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'Notwithstanding the first sentence of this Section 18.1, Intuit may freely assign this Agreement to any third party without the consent of Client', to section 18.1 is contradictary to the first line that states 'Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the other party's written consent'. This addition provides a caveat specifically for Intuit that is contradictary to the statement in the first line.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.1 Assignment. Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, ---------- without the other party's written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed); provided however, that either party may assign its rights and obligations hereunder in the event of a sale of all, or substantially all of such party's assets related to this Agreement, whether by merger, reorganization, operation of law or otherwise, or (2) either party's assignment and/or delegation of its rights and responsibilities hereunder to a wholly-owned subsidiary or joint venture in which the assigning party holds an interest. Notwithstanding the first sentence of this Section 18.1, Intuit may freely assign this Agreement to any third party without the consent of Client. Any attempt to assign this Agreement other than as permitted above will be null and void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of both parties, their successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Neither party may assign this Agreement... without the other party's written consent". The changed text adds a clause stating "Notwithstanding the first sentence of this Section 18.1, Intuit may freely assign this Agreement to any third party without the consent of Client." This creates a direct contradiction, as the first sentence requires consent for assignment, while the added sentence allows Intuit to assign the agreement without consent. This contradiction exists within the same section (18.1) and would cause confusion as to the enforceability of the original clause regarding assignment.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Sponsorship, Advertising and Exclusivity Fees. Client will pay Intuit --------------------------------------------- sponsorship and advertising fees of $2,644,010 and an exclusivity fee of $661,003. Such fees shall be paid to Intuit as follows. An initial fee of $[***] shall be due and payable on the Effective Date. The remaining --- balance of $[***] shall be paid to Intuit in 12 equal monthly installments --- of [***]. Each monthly installment should be payable in advance and due no --- later than the fifth (5th) of the month.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Sponsorship, Advertising and Exclusivity Fees. Client will pay Intuit --------------------------------------------- sponsorship and advertising fees of $2,644,010 and an exclusivity fee of $661,003. Such fees shall be paid to Intuit as follows. The entire amount of $3,305,013 shall be due and payable immediately upon the Effective Date. No other payment schedule shall apply.
perturbation.explanation: In this change, the payment schedule is altered. Originally it stated an initial fee and subsequent monthly installments. The modified section states that the entire amount is due immediately on the Effective Date, directly contradicting the installment plan previously outlined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Sponsorship, Advertising and Exclusivity Fees. Client will pay Intuit --------------------------------------------- sponsorship and advertising fees of $2,644,010 and an exclusivity fee of $661,003. Such fees shall be paid to Intuit as follows. The entire amount of $3,305,013 shall be due and payable immediately upon the Effective Date. No other payment schedule shall apply.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a payment schedule of an initial fee and subsequent monthly installments. The changed text mandates the entire amount is due immediately on the Effective Date and that no other payment schedule shall apply. This creates a direct conflict and strong contradiction, as the contradicted text cannot be reconciled with the new, immediate payment obligation. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, directly contradicts the original payment schedule that involved installments.
